summary: W004 in results.json IS the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station test. SEC-2b.7 argument is CORRECT.

results_json_win025.title: 2024 eclipse 9-station confirmation
results_json_win025.note: Placeholder entry with no data — the detailed measurements are only on W004

evidence.dome_predictions_page: No mention of W004 (only WIN-004 = SAA Cell Separation). W004 is a results.json-only identifier.
evidence.noise_floor_claim: NOT FOUND. Curmudgeon concern that dome assigns W004 to Noise Floor Limit appears unfounded. The wins.html page mentions noise floor in a separate context (W001) but W004 is explicitly titled 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication.

conclusion: The two-scoreboards argument in SEC-2b.7 is verified: the same 2024 eclipse observation is scored as confirmed_strong (W004 in results.json, sigma_distance 3.8, overshoot_ratio 1.76) while simultaneously marked [REMOVED] on the wins page (WIN-025). The observation overshoots the prediction band by 76% (-17.6 nT observed vs -10 ± 2 nT predicted), suggesting results.json pass criteria (direction_correct AND snr_sufficient) are too permissive. No correction needed to SEC-2b.7 text.
recommendation: CLOSE ISS-705 as verified. Optionally strengthen SEC-2b.7 by noting that WIN-025 also appears in results.json as a SEPARATE entry (auto_verdict: confirmed, but with null prediction/observation values) — meaning the same test appears THREE times across the dome site with three different treatments.
additional_finding: results.json has 35 entries total, all passing (35/35 = 100%). The migration from 33/34 to 35/35 noted in our text is confirmed. WIN-025 appears as a confirmed legacy entry alongside the detailed W004 replication entry.
